summary: Implement a nightly Azure Function (timer-triggered) that queries GPT-4o via TRAPI to retrieve the roster of the 1985 New York Yankees and persists the results to an Azure Storage Blob. Authentication must use Managed Identity exclusively — no API keys.
assumptions: TRAPI refers to the internal Azure OpenAI proxy/gateway (Token-based REST API Interface); endpoint and scope must be confirmed with the platform team.
Language choice defaults to Python given team familiarity with Azure Functions Python v2 programming model.
Azure Functions Consumption or Flex Consumption plan is acceptable; no VNet integration required unless TRAPI mandates it.
A single Azure Storage Account with one Blob container is sufficient for output persistence.
The nightly schedule is defined as 00:00 UTC unless otherwise specified.
Infrastructure as Code tooling defaults to Bicep.
CI/CD defaults to GitHub Actions using OIDC federated identity (no stored secrets).
Output blob is a JSON file named with the execution timestamp, e.g., 1985-ny-yankees-<ISO8601>.json.
Application Insights is used for observability; an existing Log Analytics workspace may be reused.
Plan is considered auto-approved as no explicit approval gate is noted in the spec.
open_questions: What is the TRAPI endpoint URL and required OAuth scope for GPT-4o access?
Which Azure subscription, resource group, and region should resources be provisioned in?
Should the blob be overwritten on each run or appended/versioned with a timestamp suffix?
Is there a specific Azure Functions runtime version or Python version mandated by the team?
Are there network restrictions (Private Endpoints, VNet) for TRAPI or Storage access?
What is the retention policy for blobs in the storage container?
Who owns the alert notification channel (email, Teams webhook, PagerDuty)?
Is there a cost envelope or quota for TRAPI token consumption?

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Provision all Azure infrastructure and configure Managed Identity RBAC so that subsequent development tasks have a live target environment.
entry_criteria: Azure subscription and resource group confirmed
TRAPI endpoint and OAuth scope documented
IaC toolchain (Bicep + GitHub Actions) available in repo
exit_criteria: Azure Function App, Storage Account, and Application Insights provisioned via Bicep
System-assigned Managed Identity enabled and granted Storage Blob Data Contributor + TRAPI roles
GitHub Actions OIDC federated credential configured; no secrets stored in GitHub

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Implement the timer-triggered Azure Function that retrieves the 1985 Yankees roster via TRAPI/GPT-4o and writes results to Blob Storage.
entry_criteria: Local dev environment configured with Azure CLI and Function Core Tools
exit_criteria: Function executes locally and in Azure on the nightly timer schedule
GPT-4o returns a complete 1985 Yankees roster in a structured format
Roster JSON blob written successfully to Azure Storage

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Add reliability controls, structured observability, and security hardening to production-harden the function.
entry_criteria: Core Build wave exit criteria met
Application Insights workspace live and receiving telemetry
exit_criteria: Retry policy and error handling implemented; poison/failure path logged and alerted
Structured logs emitted per invocation with correlation IDs; dashboard created
No secrets in code or config; TRAPI token acquired via Managed Identity on every invocation

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Validate end-to-end correctness, complete CI/CD pipeline, and confirm operational readiness for production go-live.
exit_criteria: All acceptance criteria from spec verified and checked off
CI pipeline passes on main branch; deployment to production is automated
Runbook complete; on-call alert routing confirmed; plan approved

tasks.title: Provision Azure infrastructure via Bicep
tasks.description: Author and deploy Bicep modules for the Azure Function App (Consumption plan), Azure Storage Account (Blob), and Application Insights workspace. Parameterize environment-specific values (region, SKU, names). Deploy via GitHub Actions using OIDC.
inputs: Azure subscription ID and resource group name
outputs: Deployed Function App, Storage Account, Application Insights resources
Bicep files committed to repo under infra/
acceptance_criteria: Bicep deploys idempotently with no errors
Function App is on Consumption or Flex Consumption plan
Storage Account has only Blob public access disabled; no public network access if TRAPI is on private network
Application Insights connected to Function App via APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING app setting
All resource names follow agreed convention and are parameterized
risks: Region availability for chosen plan type may require fallback
Quota limits on Consumption plan in target subscription

tasks.title: Configure Managed Identity and RBAC assignments
tasks.description: Enable system-assigned Managed Identity on the Function App. Assign Storage Blob Data Contributor on the target container and the required TRAPI/Azure OpenAI role (e.g., Cognitive Services OpenAI User) via Bicep roleAssignment resources. No credentials or API keys stored anywhere.
inputs: TRAPI resource ID and required role definition
acceptance_criteria: Function App Managed Identity has Storage Blob Data Contributor on the blob container
Function App Managed Identity has required TRAPI/OpenAI role
No connection strings or API keys present in app settings or code
Role assignments are defined in Bicep (not applied manually)
risks: TRAPI may require a non-standard role or an allowlist entry for the Managed Identity
Azure AD propagation delay for new role assignments can cause transient failures at first run

tasks.title: Configure GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline with OIDC
tasks.description: Set up GitHub Actions workflows for build, test, and deploy using OIDC federated credentials to authenticate to Azure. Define separate jobs for infrastructure deployment (Bicep) and function app deployment. Add branch protection to require passing CI before merge.
inputs: Azure service principal or federated credential config
outputs: OIDC federated credential configured in Azure AD
acceptance_criteria: Push to main triggers automated deploy to production Function App
No secrets stored in GitHub; only OIDC client ID, tenant ID, and subscription ID as non-secret vars
Pipeline runs unit tests and fails deploy on test failure
Bicep what-if runs on pull requests; apply only on merge to main
risks: OIDC federated credential setup requires AAD app registration permissions
If repo is org-managed, branch protection rules may need org admin approval

tasks.title: Implement TRAPI/GPT-4o client for roster retrieval
tasks.description: Implement a Python module that authenticates to TRAPI using azure-identity DefaultAzureCredential, sends a structured prompt to GPT-4o requesting the complete 1985 New York Yankees roster, and returns a parsed structured list of player names and positions.
inputs: TRAPI endpoint URL and API version
acceptance_criteria: Client acquires token via DefaultAzureCredential with no hardcoded credentials
GPT-4o prompt is deterministic and instructs model to return complete roster as JSON array
Response is validated and parsed; incomplete or malformed responses raise a typed exception
Unit tests mock the TRAPI HTTP layer and assert correct parsing for a known response fixture
Module is importable standalone and has no side effects on import
risks: GPT-4o may hallucinate or return incomplete roster; prompt engineering required to improve determinism
TRAPI token acquisition may fail if role assignment propagation is incomplete

tasks.title: Implement Azure Blob Storage writer
tasks.description: Implement a Python module that uses azure-storage-blob with DefaultAzureCredential to write the roster JSON payload to the configured container. Blob name includes ISO 8601 UTC timestamp. Overwrite behavior configurable via app setting.
inputs: Storage Account URL from app settings
outputs: Blob written to storage on each invocation
acceptance_criteria: Blob is written using Managed Identity (no connection string)
Blob name follows pattern: 1985-ny-yankees-<YYYY-MM-DDTHH-MM-SSZ>.json
Content-Type is set to application/json
Unit tests mock BlobServiceClient and assert correct blob name and payload
Storage Account URL and container name read from environment variables
risks: If container does not pre-exist, write will fail; Bicep must create container or code must handle creation

tasks.title: Implement nightly timer-triggered Azure Function
tasks.description: Wire APP-01 and APP-02 into an Azure Functions v2 Python timer-triggered function with NCRONTAB schedule '0 0 * * *' (midnight UTC). The function orchestrates retrieval and storage, logs key events, and surfaces errors for alerting.
inputs: Azure Functions Python v2 programming model docs
acceptance_criteria: Timer schedule is '0 0 * * *' (NCRONTAB) configurable via TIMER_SCHEDULE app setting
Function completes successfully in local emulator and deployed environment
Structured log entries emitted at start, after roster retrieval, and after blob write
Unhandled exceptions propagate to Application Insights as exceptions (not swallowed)
Function app starts without error on cold start
risks: Cold start latency on Consumption plan may cause timer execution delay
TRAPI latency may approach or exceed default function timeout; configure functionTimeout if needed

tasks.title: Implement retry logic and failure handling
tasks.description: Add retry logic around TRAPI calls (exponential backoff, max 3 attempts) and blob write operations. On terminal failure, log a structured error with run context and re-raise to ensure Application Insights captures the exception and triggers alerting.
inputs: tenacity or azure-core retry policy docs
outputs: Updated trapi_client.py and blob_writer.py with retry decorators
Exception taxonomy documented in code comments
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI transient errors (429, 503) are retried up to 3 times with exponential backoff
Blob write transient errors are retried up to 3 times
After max retries, exception is logged with structured fields (run_id, timestamp, error_code) and re-raised
Unit tests simulate transient failures and assert retry behavior
No silent failure paths; every exception path is logged
risks: Overly aggressive retries may exhaust TRAPI quota; respect Retry-After headers

tasks.title: Configure Application Insights monitoring and alerts
tasks.description: Define Application Insights alert rules for function failure (exception rate > 0 in a 24h window) and missed timer execution. Create a simple workbook or dashboard showing last run status, roster item count, and error rate. Configure notification action group.
acceptance_criteria: Alert fires within 5 minutes of a failed function execution
Alert fires if timer function has not executed in 26 hours (missed run detection)
Dashboard shows last successful run timestamp, player count written, and error count
All alert rules and action groups defined in Bicep (no manual portal configuration)
Notification channel tested with a manual alert trigger
risks: Notification channel owner must be confirmed before go-live
Application Insights sampling may drop low-volume exceptions; set sampling to 100% for this function

tasks.title: Security hardening and secrets audit
tasks.description: Audit all app settings, code, and pipeline configuration for hardcoded secrets or credentials. Ensure APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING is treated as a secret in Key Vault or is retrieved via Managed Identity. Run a dependency vulnerability scan on requirements.txt.
outputs: Key Vault reference for any sensitive app settings (if applicable)
acceptance_criteria: GitHub secret scanning shows zero detected secrets in repo history
pip-audit reports no high/critical CVEs in requirements.txt dependencies
No API keys, passwords, or tokens present in any tracked file
Application Insights connection string is stored as an app setting (not in code); optionally backed by Key Vault reference
All findings from audit are resolved or formally risk-accepted
risks: Transitive dependency CVEs may require version pinning that conflicts with azure-functions runtime requirements

tasks.title: End-to-end integration test and acceptance validation
tasks.description: Execute a full end-to-end test in the deployed Azure environment by triggering the function manually. Verify that the blob is written with a complete 1985 Yankees roster. Cross-reference output against a known authoritative roster list (e.g., Baseball Reference).
inputs: Azure Storage Explorer or az CLI
outputs: Test report confirming all players present in output blob
acceptance_criteria: Manual trigger of function completes without errors in Application Insights
Output blob is present in storage container with correct naming convention
Roster JSON contains all players from the official 1985 New York Yankees roster
No duplicate player entries in output
All spec acceptance criteria checkboxes can be marked complete based on test evidence
risks: GPT-4o may omit players from a large roster; prompt may need iteration before acceptance
Authoritative roster source may differ on player counts (active vs. full season roster)

tasks.title: Finalize CI/CD and deploy to production
tasks.description: Ensure the full CI/CD pipeline (build, test, Bicep deploy, function deploy) runs cleanly on the main branch. Tag a release, deploy to production, and confirm the first nightly timer execution succeeds.
outputs: First nightly execution log in Application Insights
acceptance_criteria: GitHub Actions pipeline passes all steps on merge to main
Function App in production matches deployed Bicep specification
First scheduled timer execution at 00:00 UTC succeeds and blob is written
Application Insights shows successful execution with no exceptions
Git release tag created with changelog referencing spec requirements
risks: Production TRAPI quota or firewall rules may differ from dev/test environment
Timer may not fire exactly at midnight if Function App is cold; monitor first execution window

tasks.title: Approve plan and create operational runbook
tasks.description: Obtain formal plan approval from rciapala. Author a concise operational runbook covering: how to manually trigger the function, how to inspect output blobs, how to respond to alerts, and how to redeploy. Store runbook in repo docs/.
acceptance_criteria: Plan approved by rciapala (owner/team from spec)
Runbook covers: manual trigger steps, blob inspection, alert response, and redeployment procedure
Runbook is committed to docs/runbook.md in the repository
All success criteria from spec marked complete
Monitoring and alerting confirmed active per OBS-01
risks: Approval delay blocks release readiness exit criteria
